metadata.schema: Each query has a 'primary' (the canonical answer) and a 'ground_truth' list (all acceptable matches). Easy queries keep ground_truth=[primary] since they are direct term lookups.
metadata.description: Benchmark for Corpus vector retrieval. v2.0 upgrades to multi-concept ground truth enabling Precision@K and nDCG. Hard tier rewritten with reasoning-based queries.

Mathematical representation of data as fixed-length numeric vectors for similarity computation.
Breaking long documents into smaller pieces to fit within the context window.
AI framework that retrieves external knowledge to ground language model responses.
Autonomous system that plans tasks, uses tools, and maintains memory across steps.
Using a pre-trained language model as a starting point and adapting it to a specific task.
Mechanism that allows each token in a sequence to attend to every other token.
Splitting model weight matrix into two smaller low-rank matrices to reduce training parameters.
Injecting retrieved context snippets into a prompt before generating an LLM response.
Specialized database designed to perform fast approximate nearest neighbor searches.
Process of training language models using human-ranked preference data and a reward model.
The technique of running multiple attention heads in parallel to capture different subspace representations.
Direct alignment technique that optimizes model from binary preferences without RL training loop.
Technique to re-score retrieved documents before passing top results to the generator.
Combining dense vector retrieval with sparse keyword retrieval for improved recall.
A lightweight model adapter approach enabling fine-tuning with a fraction of parameters.
Encoding position information into token representations for sequence-order awareness.
Aggregation mechanism in transformers that reads information from all encoder hidden states.
Algorithm for text retrieval that scores documents using term frequency and inverse document frequency.
Extracting document structure, headers, metadata tags, and entities from raw text files.
Framework to validate whether LLM answers are supported by retrieved source documents.
A supervised learning technique that fits a straight line to predict continuous output values.
An ensemble method that trains many decision trees and combines their predictions by majority vote.
Reducing high-dimensional data to fewer dimensions while preserving variance and structure.
A recurrent network architecture with gating mechanisms designed to retain long-range dependencies.
A technique that applies a learned filter over local regions of an input to detect spatial features.
A penalty added to the training loss to prevent a model from fitting noise in training data.
A value-based RL algorithm that learns the optimal action for each state by iterating over a Q-table.
A topology-preserving dimensionality reduction method that scales better than t-SNE on large datasets.
A CNN operation that downsamples feature maps by taking the maximum value in each local window.
A layer in a CNN that captures spatial hierarchies by applying learned filters across input patches.

What is the difference between measuring whether an answer is faithful versus whether it is complete?

How does the maximum input length of a language model constrain document retrieval strategy?

What framework allows stateful multi-agent workflows with conditional branching and cycle support?
How can a model generalize to unseen tasks by conditioning on a few labelled examples in the prompt?
Which retrieval strategy restores surrounding context by merging child chunks with their parent node?
How does subword tokenization balance vocabulary size with coverage of rare and compound words?
How does a production RAG pipeline ensure generated answers stay grounded in source documents?
How does an agent decide when to reason internally versus when to invoke an external tool?
Which component of the Transformer architecture scales dot-product scores before applying softmax?

What framework provides automated metrics for RAG quality including context precision and answer faithfulness?
How does structured output from an LLM enable integration with downstream APIs and databases?
What sequence of signals indicates a deployed model has drifted and requires retraining?
How does a tree-based model decide which feature to split on at each node to maximise class separation?
How does t-SNE preserve local cluster structure when projecting high-dimensional embeddings into 2D?
How does a gated recurrent unit decide what information to forget and what to pass forward?
Why does XGBoost outperform vanilla gradient boosting on structured data in competitive benchmarks?
How does a DQN stabilise Q-value estimation using a replay buffer and a target network?
